item.title: Gisele Bündchen celebrates birthday in leopard-print bikini as she reflects on gratitude
item.description: Gisele Bündchen stunned in a leopard-print bikini for her 46th birthday on Instagram, with husband Joaquim Valente and Heidi Klum sending love.
item.content:encoded:
Gisele Bündchen is celebrating her birthday in style.

The Brazilian supermodel took to Instagram to celebrate her 46th birthday with a series of photos, including one of her lounging in a leopard-print bikini.

"Thank you all for the love and birthday wishes. I feel so grateful for this new cycle that is beginning and for all the adventures that lie ahead," she captioned the post. "May the love and kindness you share return to each of you a thousandfold. ❤️✨"

In the photo, Bündchen is seen smiling as she lounges on a green lounge chair and holding a bunch of pink and white balloons.

SYDNEY SWEENEY SHARES BIKINI AND UNDERWEAR PHOTOS IN INSTAGRAM DUMP FROM AUSTRALIA TRIP

Other photos in the post showed the mom of three looking up at the sun with her hand on her chest and her eyes closed, as well as a shot of her birthday cake, which was in the shape of a heart.

Her husband, Joaquim Valente, showed her some love in the comments section, writing, "👏🎉✨❤️🥳."

Others joined in to wish Bündchen a happy birthday, with one writing, "Hope you had the happiest birthday 🎂 🎉," and another adding, "Happy birthday my beautiful angel🎉🥳🪽." Fellow supermodel, Heidi Klum also sent her well wishes, writing, "Happy Birthday G🐐❤️."

The celebratory post comes just a few days after Bündchen shared photos from her recent family getaway to Tahiti, which included snaps of her older children Benjamin Rein, 16, and Vivian Lake, 13, whom she shares with her ex-husband Tom Brady, 48, and rare photos of her with her youngest child who she shares with Valente.

CLICK HERE TO SIGN UP FOR THE ENTERTAINMENT NEWSLETTER

She and Valente announced they were expecting their first child together in October 2024, with outlets reporting their son was born in February 2025.

Following the birth of their son, Bündchen and Valente tied the knot during a private ceremony in Surfside, Florida in December 2025.

Just last month, the model shared a series of photos of Valente spending time with all three of her children, in honor of Father's Day, calling him "an incredible role model" in the caption.

"Happy Father’s Day @joaquimvalente !Thank you for leading by example, and for the values you embody, love, humility, integrity, discipline, kindness and consistency," she wrote. "You are an incredible role model. We are all so grateful for you, and we love you so much."

LIKE WHAT YOU'RE READING? CLICK HERE FOR MORE ENTERTAINMENT NEWS

item.title: Israeli UN envoy blasts Mamdani as 'clown' over call to arrest Netanyahu: 'It's a stunt'
item.description: Israel's UN ambassador called Mayor Zohran Mamdani a "clown" after he demanded federal authorities arrest Benjamin Netanyahu on an ICC warrant.
item.content:encoded:
Israel’s ambassador to the United Nations on Wednesday blasted New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani as a "clown" for demanding federal authorities arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu when Netanyahu arrives in the city.

Danny Danon’s remarks came after Mamdani accused Netanyahu of being a "war criminal" and the "architect of a horrific genocide" against Palestinians while calling on the federal government to execute an International Criminal Court arrest warrant against the Israeli leader.

Netanyahu is scheduled to address the United Nations General Assembly in Manhattan in September.

NETANYAHU FIRES BACK AT NYC MAYOR MAMDANI'S VOW TO ENFORCE 'BOGUS ARREST WARRANT'

"Those baseless remarks about harassing the prime minister — it's a joke, and he's a clown," Danon told Fox News Digital Wednesday.

"Everyone knows it's a show. It's a stunt," Danon added. "A mayor should be dealing with the homeless people, the garbage in New York, and leave foreign affairs to the people in Washington who are elected to do it. He should take care of the security of the people in New York, especially the Jewish community, which is seeing the rise of antisemitism. That is his first responsibility."

The YouTube channel for the New York City Mayor's Office released a video July 21, "Benjamin Netanyahu is a war criminal."

"Benjamin Netanyahu is a war criminal, the architect of a horrific genocide against the Palestinian people," Mamdani said, repeating comments he made in an interview with The New York Times.

Mamdani leveled a series of allegations against the Israeli leader, claiming he was "responsible for the killing of more than 73,000 people, for the maiming of tens of thousands of children, as those who survive undergo amputation without anesthesia."

He also accused Netanyahu of targeting neonatal hospitals and maternity care centers and alleged that he blocked food and humanitarian aid.

CNN LEGAL ANALYST CALLS OUT 'UTTERLY RIDICULOUS' CLAIM BY MAMDANI ABOUT NETANYAHU ARREST

"Just last month, the U.N. confirmed that Palestinian children continue to be deliberately targeted and killed by Israeli armed forces, more than eight months after the so-called ceasefire," Mamdani said. 

"All as we, as Americans, pay for the bombs that do the killing. There is a reason the International Criminal Court issued a warrant for his arrest."

Danon pushed back sharply against the mayor's accusations.

"There is a lot of humanitarian aid coming into Gaza every day," Danon argued.

"Nobody speaks about those issues anymore, but he wants to resume spreading blood libels, promoting the claims of genocide, starvation and all those lies that were spread by him, by his wife and by other people during the war.

"He wants to go back to this situation, but today there are no humanitarian issues in Gaza," Danon added.

"Mamdani continues to promote those blood libels against Israel and against the Jews."

Mamdani also said that City Hall cannot act on the warrant unilaterally.

"It is clear that we do not have the independent legal authority to enforce this warrant. The federal government, however, does, and I call on them to join the ICC and execute this warrant," Mamdani said.

"Benjamin Netanyahu is not welcome in New York City, nor is any other war criminal at large."

MAMDANI’S ‘DERANGED’ ISRAEL TAKE RESURFACES AS HE TARGETS NETANYAHU

Israeli officials strongly deny all charges. Netanyahu’s office has rejected the ICC’s jurisdiction, labeling it "a kangaroo court that has no jurisdiction over Americans or Israelis."

"People are smart; they know there's no legal basis for his remarks," Danon said. "The U.S. is not part of the ICC. There is no warrant that is actually effective against the prime minister in the U.S. So it's a political stunt."

"We have serious issues to deal with — the war with Iran and other critical matters.

"The prime minister is not actually dealing with Mamdani's remarks at all. He said it very clearly: He intends to come and speak at the U.N.

"Prime Minister Netanyahu will come to New York and will speak in the General Assembly," Danon said. "The only thing the mayor can do is come to the U.N., grab a seat, listen to the Prime Minister and maybe learn something from his speech.

"Last year, Mayor Adams came to the prime minister's speech," Danon noted. "A local leader can come and hear. That's the only thing he can do."

item.title: Burger King makes major changes after thousands of customers sounded off
item.description: Burger King introduced the Your Way Champion role and Whopper Guarantee after thousands of customer calls demanding better order accuracy and service.
item.content:encoded:
Visitors to Burger King may soon notice several changes inspired by updates that customers of the fast-food chain said they wanted.

"Earlier this year, we opened our phone lines and invited people to share their honest feedback, and they did," a Burger King representative told Fox News Digital.

"One of the biggest takeaways was that our guests want to feel welcomed when they walk in, know their order will be made the way they asked and have confidence that if something isn't right, we'll make it right."

BURGER KING FANS SWEAR A SIMPLE TRICK GETS YOU A FRESHER WHOPPER, BUT NOT EVERYONE IS CONVINCED

Burger King said the initiative generated thousands of customer phone calls and text messages.

Four months later, the company is introducing two strategies aimed at improving customer service and order accuracy.

The Your Way Champion is a "reimagined restaurant manager role designed to empower restaurant leaders and put the guest experience first," Burger King announced in a news release. The champion will wear a dedicated uniform and serve as a point person for customers, overseeing order customization and satisfaction.

The Whopper Guarantee promises that if a Whopper, Burger King's signature hamburger, doesn't meet a customer's expectations, "the brand will not only continue to remake it on the spot, but they'll offer the guest's next Whopper free of charge," redeemable with a QR code.

TEST YOURSELF WITH OUR LATEST LIFESTYLE QUIZ

"This is about bringing 'Have It Your Way' to life in every restaurant, every day," Burger King told Fox News Digital. 

"The Your Way Champion puts hospitality front and center, while the Whopper Guarantee gives guests confidence we'll stand behind our flagship product being served just the way they want it. Together, they're about earning trust one visit at a time."

Burger King's announcement of the changes drew varied reactions from social media users.

"Listening to your guests and acting on their feedback is what builds long-term loyalty," one user wrote on LinkedIn. "Excited to see Burger King continuing to invest in both the guest experience and the restaurant teams that make it happen every day!"

CLICK HERE TO SIGN UP FOR OUR LIFESTYLE NEWSLETTER

"Love to see a brand that actually listens to its guests," another wrote on the platform. "The 'Your Way Champion' role + Whopper Guarantee is a brilliant move. This is how you raise the bar in QSR [quick service restaurants]. Excited to experience it!"

"Shout out to a couple of champions," a Facebook user wrote of a recent experience at a Burger King in Canada. 

"I don't think I have ever seen two people enjoy doing their job and looking after the customers as I did. Aalisha and Erwin were both happy, personable and an absolute delight to observe while I sat in this super clean restaurant. I have been in the hospitality business for over 20 years and am hard to impress ... but they did it. Well done."

A couple of other social media users expressed disappointment, claiming Burger King is prioritizing its customers over its employees.

"Burger King is the worst place to work for," one person wrote on LinkedIn. "I've been there seven and a half years, and I'm a breakfast coordinator. I make $16.35 an hour, and they refuse to give me a raise, which is bull crap."

"We don't make that much and work way too hard to be doing all that," someone else wrote on Instagram. "I've noticed this company really likes their customers more than their own employees. It's sad."

Several other commenters chimed in on specific menu items they have missed or would like to be brought back or changed. Many users expressed love for the Whopper, which Burger King describes as "America's favorite burger" and "the brand's most beloved item."

"Actually, I don't care about the service, I just really like the Whopper!" someone wrote on Facebook.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE LIFESTYLE NEWS

Burger King will continue gathering feedback and using it to "turn those insights into meaningful improvements across our restaurants," the chain told Fox News Digital.

item.title: CDC reveals massive behind-the-scenes public health operation that kept FIFA World Cup fans safe
item.description: The CDC operated behind the scenes during the FIFA World Cup, deploying disease surveillance, wastewater monitoring and real-time data dashboards.
item.content:encoded:
FIRST ON FOX: The World Cup tested not only the players on the pitch, but the Trump administration's large public health operation to safeguard the public as fans from all over the world moved across the United States.

Amid the fandom, as 11 American cities hosted 39 teams and 78 matches, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) operated largely behind the scenes to detect emerging threats and to respond to public health emergencies.

"Readiness is not only measured by how we respond to emergencies, but by the work we do every day to prevent them," acting CDC Director Dr. Jay Bhattacharya said in a statement to Fox News Digital. 

"The FIFA World Cup demonstrated the strength of CDC’s partnerships, preparedness and commitment to protecting the health of every community we serve."

CONGO TEAM MUST ISOLATE TO ENTER UNITED STATES FOR WORLD CUP AMID EBOLA OUTBREAK

In total, the FIFA World Cup hosted 48 teams across 16 cities in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, with more than 5 million tickets sold.

An estimated 6.5 million fans from around the world converged in the three host countries, bringing their passion, enthusiasm and, possibly, germs. Overlapping with Independence Day celebrations, large gatherings were common, as well as the backdrop of concurrent international disease activity, including the Ebola and Hantavirus outbreaks.

Infectious disease can easily spread in large crowds, and experts were already looking at wastewater, a possible uptick in hospital visits and social media for any signs of a potential outbreak.

Diseases like measles are always a top concern. As of July 16, there were 2,260 confirmed measles cases in the U.S., according to the CDC.

"The matches drew the world's attention. The work that kept them healthy did not — and that was the point," the CDC said in a news release Sunday.

FBI BOSS KASH PATEL DETAILS HOW AGENCY AND PARTNERS SECURED RECORD-SHATTERING FIFA WORLD CUP

The CDC's effort included a World Cup data dashboard that provided health departments with real-time data on disease trends across multiple cities as fans and teams traveled throughout the tournament, as well as around-the-clock health operations at U.S. ports of entry in coordination with U.S. Customs and Border Protection to monitor international passengers.

The agency also deployed its experts to strengthen disease surveillance, laboratory capacity, wastewater surveillance and preparedness planning in host cities.

In all, the agency coordinated across a broad network, including multiple CDC divisions, the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), the Administration for Strategic Preparedness and Response (ASPR) and the White House FIFA World Cup Task Force.

WHITE HOUSE ADDRESSES GROWING PARASITE OUTBREAK AFTER MORE THAN 400 AMERICANS SICKENED

In March, the CDC released guidance providing health officials with a framework for preparing for a broad range of threats, including biological, chemical, radiologic/nuclear, environmental and mass casualty incidents.

The guidance encouraged every jurisdiction to approach the tournament through a risk-based lens, recognizing that travel between venues connects communities far beyond the host cities.

The tournament is over, but the work is not. The same preparedness, surveillance and partnerships that supported the World Cup are part of CDC's readiness and response mission every day of the year, continuing through the close of the activation on July 31 and well beyond.

item.title: Big things brewing off the field in Tampa thanks to Jake Browning's wife & her plans to upgrade her fit checks
item.description: Stephanie Niles is learning to sew her own game-day outfits after joining the Tampa Bay Buccaneers as Jake Browning's newly married NFL wife.
item.content:encoded:
What an offseason it has been for Stephanie Niles. After leaving it all on the field for her last fit check as a member of the Cincinnati Bengals, she has landed with a new team and added a new ring.

That's right, with Jake Browning signing with the Tampa Bay Buccaneers and the two of them tying the knot, it's been a busy few months for the couple as they prepare for the quarterback's eighth season in the NFL.

But the practice squad girlfriend turned backup quarterback fiancée has big plans for the upcoming season after officially becoming an NFL wife. Now that she's introduced herself at the local country club, she's getting to work.

OLIVIA CULPO PULLS A VETERAN MOVE BY WISHING CHRISTIAN MCCAFFREY A HAPPY BIRTHDAY WITH STRING BIKINI PICS

Mrs. Browning is giving her closet an upgrade this season and has already started working on her outfit for the Buccaneers home opener. She's going big or settling for a T-shirt from the team shop.

Her obsession began, she admits, with the viral bodysuit she wore a couple of years ago in Cincinnati. Ever since that moment, she's been preparing as if she's a starter and made the most of every opportunity.

That's taken Stephanie Browning to a new level when it comes to her approach to her game day fits. Her ideas have surpassed simply putting her trust in an "alarming little amount of thread."

She announced on Wednesday that she's learning to sew and the rest of the league has officially been put on notice.

RAMS' SEAN MCVAY AND HIS MODEL WIFE PUT THE REST OF THE NFL ON NOTICE WITH NOTHING BUT SWIMSUITS ON

What a veteran move. When you feel like you've done all you can with your current skill set, you add some more skills.

That's how you make it off the practice squad and onto a roster. That's how you land a backup role and start some games when the starter goes down.

I don't want to toot my own horn here, but I hit the nail on the head with the "big things brewing off the field in Tampa" proclamation. If you're new to the league or haven’t found your footing yet, take notes.

A well-timed viral moment can only take you so far. You have to be prepared for what comes next, and Stephanie Browning has taken it all in stride.

item.title: Newsom turns to Hunter Biden’s high-powered ex-lawyer as federal scrutiny closes in on his family, allies
item.description: Gavin Newsom hired Abbe Lowell, Hunter Biden's former attorney, as federal investigators scrutinize Jennifer Siebel Newsom's nonprofit finances.
item.content:encoded:
California Gov. Gavin Newsom and his wife, Jennifer Siebel Newsom, have turned to Hunter Biden’s former attorney as federal investigators scrutinize financial and nonprofit matters involving the first partner and a separate federal fraud case has ensnared the governor’s former chief of staff.

The hiring of prominent Washington defense lawyer Abbe Lowell, whose recent clients have included high-profile figures who have clashed with Trump, including New York Attorney General Letitia James and former President Joe Biden’s son, comes as Newsom dismisses the scrutiny as a politically motivated "fishing expedition" aimed at one of President Donald Trump’s most visible Democratic opponents. 

In a news conference Tuesday, the California governor confirmed he had obtained Lowell's counsel.

GAVIN NEWSOM CLAIMS TRUMP ORDERED DOJ PROBE TARGETING HIM AND HIS WIFE

Lowell, a veteran white-collar defense attorney who served as House Democrats’ chief minority counsel during former President Bill Clinton’s impeachment, has represented a bipartisan roster of political heavyweights over his decades-long career. 

His past clients include Hunter Biden, Trump’s daughter Ivanka Trump and son-in-law Jared Kushner, former Democratic Sens. Bob Menendez and John Edwards and an employee of longtime Trump ally Tom Barrack.

More recently, Lowell has represented several prominent figures who say they have been targeted by the Trump administration, including Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook, former Department of Homeland Security official Miles Taylor and whistleblower attorney Mark Zaid. 

Lowell did not respond to Fox News Digital's questions in time for publication.

At least two criminal investigations connected to Newsom’s family and political orbit have been handled by the U.S. Attorney’s Office for the Eastern District of California, according to CalMatters. Citing a Justice Department source familiar with the matter, the outlet reported that both originated with whistleblowers and people in Sacramento.

WHAT TO KNOW ABOUT THE NEWSOM-LINKED CHARITIES REPORTEDLY CAUGHT IN DOJ’S SIGHTS

One inquiry reportedly involves tax-related scrutiny involving Jennifer Siebel Newsom and financial arrangements connected to her nonprofit and filmmaking work. The other centered on former Newsom chief of staff Dana Williamson, who later pleaded guilty in a separate federal fraud case that prosecutors say grew out of an investigation launched during the Biden administration.

The Los Angeles Times reported that nonprofits and a for-profit production company founded by Siebel Newsom are at the center of the federal scrutiny into her charity and business finances. The Justice Department and FBI have not commented publicly on the investigation, according to the Times. The Justice Department did not respond to Fox News Digital’s questions in time for publication.

Among the organizations identified by the Times is the Representation Project, a nonprofit that federal tax records show has paid Siebel Newsom more than $1.9 million in compensation. The nonprofit also awarded roughly $2.2 million in contracts to Girls Club Entertainment, a for-profit production company separately owned by Siebel Newsom.

CRUZ PUSHES BILL TO HOLD TAX-EXEMPT SPONSORS ACCOUNTABLE AS DOJ PROBES SINGHAM NETWORK

The Times also identified the California Partners Project, another nonprofit founded by Siebel Newsom. State disclosures reviewed by the outlet show it has received nearly $5.1 million in "behested payments" reported by the governor since 2020. Behested payments are donations made at an elected official’s request for charitable, legislative or governmental purposes rather than contributions to the official’s political campaign.

Newsom and his wife have maintained that the current Justice Department is weaponizing investigations in retaliation for the pair's criticism of Trump and his national political profile. However, in an interview last week with MS NOW, Siebel Newsom was asked about the origin of the probes and said she did not know the details.

Newsom's office did not respond to Fox News Digital's questions about whether he has been contacted by any federal investigators in relation to these cases or any others. However, Newsom did tell KCRA-3 in California during a news conference Tuesday he believes it is just a matter of time until he does.

"It's inevitable, come on," Newsom said when asked about any contact from federal investigators. "Look at who we're dealing with."

Fox News Digital reached out to the White House, which referred all questions to the Justice Department.

item.title: Travelers unite in powerful moment of silence as fallen US service member returns home
item.description: Video captured hundreds of travelers at Raleigh-Durham International Airport standing in silence during a dignified transfer. The clip gained attention online.
item.content:encoded:
A powerful display of respect unfolded inside a busy North Carolina airport as strangers united to honor a fallen American service member's final journey home.

Video shared by the White House and many others on social media shows a large group of travelers gathered along the terminal windows at Raleigh-Durham International Airport, many with their hands over their hearts, during the dignified transfer of a fallen service member on the tarmac below.

"BEAUTIFUL MOMENT: Hundreds of Americans at Raleigh-Durham International Airport stood in silence to honor a fallen service member as they returned home," the White House wrote Wednesday in a post on Instagram. "A grateful nation remembers. God bless our troops."

The moving tribute quickly drew an outpouring of emotional reactions online, with many commenters praising the travelers who paused their own journeys to pay their respects.

GOLD STAR WIDOW SAYS 'BLESSINGS ARE INFINITE' AFTER STRANGERS FLOOD HUSBAND'S ARLINGTON GRAVE

"God bless and comfort these families. I am so thankful for their service. The dark will not outshine the light!" one person wrote.

"There is hope for the USA. God bless our fallen heroes," another commenter added.

AS AN ARMY WIDOW, I WILL NEVER FORGET HOW ORDINARY AMERICANS HONORED MY HUSBAND

Others said the tribute left them emotional.

"This brought me to tears," one person wrote.

The United Service Organizations (USO) confirmed to Fox News Digital that its Raleigh-Durham Airport Center Honors Support Team assisted with a dignified transfer at the airport on July 21 but declined to identify the fallen service member.

PENTAGON IDENTIFIES ARMY SOLDIER KILLED IN ACTION DURING IRAQ OPERATION

"Out of respect for the service member, the family and military notification procedures, we are unable to provide additional information regarding the individual or the transfer," the USO said.

The organization said its staff and volunteers are prepared to support military families during some of their most difficult moments.

"When a service member makes their final journey home, our staff and volunteers are prepared to provide compassionate support to families and work alongside our military partners to help ensure a dignified transfer and the respectful honoring of our nation’s fallen," the USO said.

The organization added that it works to ease logistical burdens and provide families with a welcoming space and essential services so they can focus on honoring their loved one.

The airport tribute came as the nation mourned four U.S. service members recently killed during the war with Iran.

Earlier Wednesday, the Department of War's Rapid Response account announced that four American service members who "made the ultimate sacrifice for our nation" had been welcomed home during dignified transfer ceremonies.

"Today, we welcomed home four brave Americans who made the ultimate sacrifice for our nation," the account wrote on X. "Their courage will never be forgotten."

FBI Director Kash Patel also paid tribute to the four fallen service members in a post on X.

"We owe the ultimate costs to our American patriots that paid the ultimate price for this country," Patel wrote. "We will never forget."

Patel named 1st Lt. Tyler Feehan, Sgt. Michael Swinton, Sgt. Angel Rampersad and Pvt. Isabella Gonzales, adding, "Or their families."

It is not immediately clear whether the White House video from the airport depicts one of those transfers.

The four are among 18 U.S. service members killed during the war with Iran.

On Tuesday, the Pentagon identified Army Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton, 30, of Fayetteville, North Carolina, as one of the latest U.S. service members to die while deployed, announcing he was killed during an operation at Erbil Air Base in Iraq involving a downed Iranian attack drone.

North Carolina Senate candidate Michael Whatley honored Swinton in a statement provided to Fox News Digital.

"May God Bless Sgt. Michael Swinton and his family," Whatley said. "North Carolina and the country owe an immeasurable debt to those who have given their lives to protect and preserve the liberties which make America the greatest nation on Earth."

Rep. Mark Harris, R-N.C., also mourned Swinton and Feehan while pointing to the airport tribute as a reflection of the nation’s gratitude.

"North Carolina and the Fort Bragg community mourn the loss of two remarkable individuals who made the ultimate sacrifice for our freedom," Harris said in a statement to Fox News Digital.

"Beth and I are praying for the families, friends, and loved ones of Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton and 1st Lt. Tyler James Feehan.

"As today’s beautiful moment at Raleigh-Durham International Airport showed, we will never forget the debt our nation owes these two heroes."

Fox News Digital's Eric Mack contributed to this report.

item.title: Experts reveal how to avoid deadly Legionnaires' disease as NYC outbreak kills 5
item.description: The New York City Legionnaires' disease outbreak has killed five and sickened 82, but doctors say key prevention steps can reduce exposure risk.
item.content:encoded:
As New York City continues to investigate an outbreak of Legionnaires' disease that has claimed multiple lives and sickened dozens of people, doctors say there are ways people can reduce the risk of exposure.

As of July 21, there have been five deaths and 82 diagnoses linked to the Upper East Side outbreak, according to the New York City Department of Health and Mental Hygiene. Eight patients remain hospitalized.

Health officials said no new cases had been reported in the previous six days, suggesting the source of exposure has likely been eliminated, although the investigation remains ongoing.

NYC LEGIONNAIRES' DISEASE OUTBREAK KILLS FOURTH VICTIM; 76 CASES REPORTED

Legionnaires’ disease is a serious type of pneumonia that is typically spread by tiny water droplets containing Legionella bacteria, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

Common sources of infection include contaminated building water systems, such as cooling towers (large structures that remove heat from water used in building cooling systems), hot tubs or plumbing. Cases have also been reported on cruise ships and in spas. It is not generally spread from person to person, according to the CDC.

"We get infected when it gets aerosolized in the water droplets, usually, and we breathe those droplets in," Dr. Dana Mordue, associate professor of pathology, microbiology and immunology at New York Medical College, told Fox News Digital.

NYC HEALTH OFFICIALS WARN CENTRAL PARK VISITORS AFTER LEGIONNAIRES’ OUTBREAK GROWS ON UPPER EAST SIDE

"You can also get infected if you have water that's been contaminated, and you aspirate it and swallow it into your lungs, but most often you get it from air droplets that are contaminated from various types of water sources."

Although it is possible to contract Legionnaires’ in the home, outbreaks are almost always linked to exposure to water droplets coming from a water system in a "commercial-type building," according to Mordue.

"The good news is you usually don't get it from the types of cooling systems that we have in our cars or our homes. But if you don't clean your humidifiers, technically any water source can get contaminated with Legionella."

The most widespread symptoms of Legionnaires' disease include cough, shortness of breath, fever, muscle aches and headaches, according to the CDC. Some people may also experience diarrhea, nausea and confusion.

Symptoms typically begin two to 14 days after exposure to the bacteria, although they can appear later. The illness often requires treatment with antibiotics.

"The mortality rate is not particularly high for this disease if you are immune-competent and young and healthy," said Mordue.

Risk is higher for older adults, smokers and people with compromised immune systems, "a lot of the same risk factors you might see for COVID," the doctor noted.

The CDC shares the following measures to prevent the spread of Legionnaires’ disease.

For anyone who may have been exposed and developed symptoms, it is important to seek immediate medical care, doctors say. Early diagnosis and antibiotic treatment can reduce the risk of severe illness.

"If you have certain risk factors, particularly advanced age, get help as quickly as you can," Mordue advised.

item.title: Cole Hauser admits Taylor Sheridan's absence from writers' room created 'challenges' on 'Dutton Ranch'
item.description: The "Yellowstone" actor admitted "everything had changed" on "Dutton Ranch" after Taylor Sheridan stepped back, saying the cast had to "figure out" a new creative team.
item.content:encoded:
Cole Hauser is giving fans a glimpse behind the scenes of "Dutton Ranch" — and it wasn't all smooth riding.

The actor, who reprises his fan-favorite role as Rip Wheeler in the "Yellowstone" spin-off, acknowledged the cast faced an uphill battle after franchise creator Taylor Sheridan stepped away from the show's writers' room during the spin-off’s first season.

"There were challenges, you know?" Hauser told Film Inside NYC. "Not having Taylor [Sheridan] there, for us to really kind of be able to work with these new writers, these new directors, these people... Everything had changed."

ED HARRIS SAYS HE WAS READY TO LEAVE DUTTON RANCH AFTER FEELING MISLED ABOUT HIS ROLE

"The only… normality that we had whatsoever was Carter [played by Finn Little], Christina [director Alexandra Voros] … Kelly and myself," he added. 

"Dutton Ranch," which wrapped its first season earlier this month, marked the first series in the "Yellowstone" universe not to be written by Sheridan. 

While the franchise creator remained on board as an executive producer, day-to-day creative duties shifted to showrunner Chad Feehan and an entirely new writing staff. 

For Hauser, the transition wasn't seamless. 

"I enjoy a challenge, it certainly was a challenge," he said. "We worked our a--es off this year... And I don't think a lot of the outside noise or the thought of, like, 'What's the weight of the show?' was really apparent to me at the time."

LIKE WHAT YOU’RE READING? CLICK HERE FOR MORE ENTERTAINMENT NEWS

Rather than dwell on the expectations surrounding one of television's biggest franchises, Hauser said the cast focused on finding its footing with an unfamiliar creative team.

The actor pointed out that the experience ultimately strengthened the cast.

TAYLOR SHERIDAN’S 'YELLOWSTONE’ FORMULA TURNS COUNTRY STARS INTO SCENE-STEALERS AS MORGAN WADE MAKES HER DEBUT

"It was about putting my head down and working my a-- off with… what I had around me. The writers that we had, the new writers that we had, that we had to figure out on our feet how to really perform and tell a story. So, I'm proud of what we accomplished, and I look forward to doing it again."

Hauser's comments come after speculation surrounding the show's creative direction.

CLICK HERE TO SIGN UP FOR THE ENTERTAINMENT NEWSLETTER

The franchise faced scrutiny following blunt comments from co-star Ed Harris, who said he was disappointed by how his character, Everett McKinney, was handled during Season 1.

Speaking to Variety, Harris said, "Midway through this first season, I was ready to say, 'Get me the f--- out of here,' to tell you the truth... I didn't feel I was being used... I felt a little bit misled."

Harris claimed he signed on believing Everett would be one of the show's central characters, but felt that promise never materialized.

"Prior to signing on for it, I talked quite a bit about what the season was and what my character was going to do. That I was one of the four main characters. And that wasn't really the case," Harris said, adding that he also was disappointed a scene in which his character sang was ultimately cut from the final edit.

The Oscar-nominated actor said producers have assured him his role will expand when production resumes.

"We don't start [Season 2] until February," Harris said. "And I'm being told that my character will have a little bit more to do. It just was a little frustrating, to tell you the truth."

item.title: Trump gives emotional tribute to fallen Army Lt Tyler Feehan after flying grieving family on Air Force One
item.description: President Trump revealed during a Georgia speech that he invited fallen soldier Tyler Feehan's grieving family aboard Air Force One after the dignified transfer at Dover Air Force Base.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump paid an emotional tribute to fallen Army 1st Lt. Tyler James Feehan during an event Wednesday at Wheeler High School in Marietta, Georgia, revealing he invited the slain soldier's grieving family aboard Air Force One. 

The family was in the crowd after Trump met with them earlier in the day at Dover Air Force Base in Delaware.

Trump traveled to Georgia after attending the dignified transfer ceremony of four U.S. service members killed in Jordan, including Feehan, and the president met Feehan's parents, Shari and Stephen, and his uncle David.

Speaking to the crowd, Trump recounted learning the family was also headed to Georgia after the ceremony.

TRUMP TAKES INAUGURAL FLIGHT ABOARD NEW AIR FORCE ONE AHEAD OF LIBRARY DEBUT HONORING FAMED OUTDOORSMAN

"I said, 'If you want, I'll give you a ride on Air Force One,'" Trump told the audience. "Today they were on Air Force One, and it's quite nice, I must say."

Trump said the family was "heartbroken" but grateful for the gesture.

"They couldn't believe it," he said. "They live minutes away. We're here today."

ARMY IDS 2 VICTIMS KILLED IN JORDAN AIR BASE ATTACK; TRUMP: IRAN WILL PAY 'MANY TIMES OVER'

Feehan, 25, of Ewa Beach, Hawaii, was among four U.S. service members killed in Jordan while supporting a U.S. mission. He was assigned to the 2nd Battalion, 55th Air Defense Artillery Regiment, 32nd Army Air and Missile Defense Command at Fort Bragg, North Carolina.

Pvt. Isabella Gonzales, 19, of Carrollton, Texas, was also among those killed. U.S. Central Command announced the deaths Saturday.

Trump said he reviews biographies of fallen service members before each dignified transfer and described Feehan as an exceptional young officer with a promising future.

DEMOCRATIC MARYLAND GOV WES MOORE PRAISES TRUMP ACCOUNTS AS ‘SMART POLICY’

"Lieutenant Tyler Feehan ... he was a person that was going all the way," Trump said. "He was going to be a general. He would have been a general very quickly. He was heading in that path."

Trump then thanked Feehan's family, whom he said he got to know during the flight from Dover to Georgia aboard Air Force One.

"But I just want to thank Tyler's mother, Shari; his father, Stephen; and his uncle David. Great people. I got to know them a little bit on the plane and standing there and watching this site," he said.

Calling dignified transfers the most difficult responsibility of the presidency, Trump said he would never forget the family.

"I think it's the toughest thing I do," Trump said. "But to Shari and Stephen and David, we will carry Tyler's memory in our hearts forever. I just want to thank you. It's an honor to have you here today."

The audience responded with sustained applause before Trump addressed Feehan's mother directly.

"Shari, he's looking down right here," Trump said. "He's so proud right now."

Trump visited Georgia on Wednesday to tout his administration's Trump Accounts initiative, saying the program would help families build long-term savings for their children as part of what he described as America's "Golden Age."

item.title: Manhunt underway after masked gunman allegedly shoots NYC cab driver in face during overnight robbery
item.description: A masked gunman allegedly shot a 21-year-old livery cab driver in the face during an attempted robbery in Brooklyn before fleeing with cash, according to police.
item.content:encoded:
Police in New York City are searching for a masked gunman who allegedly shot a 21-year-old cab driver in the face during an overnight armed robbery in Brooklyn.

According to a New York Police Department (NYPD) statement obtained by Fox 5 New York, the incident happened around 4 a.m. Saturday when the suspect targeted a TLC-licensed livery cab, a vehicle typically used for pre-arranged rides rather than street hails.

Authorities said the suspect entered the vehicle and allegedly threatened the driver, demanding money and the keys to the cab.

NYPD DETECTIVE, CHILDREN SHOT IN SEPARATE BROOKLYN SHOOTINGS OVER HOLIDAY WEEKEND

The suspect then opened fire as the driver attempted to exit the vehicle, striking him in the face.

"The victim proceeded to exit the vehicle when the unidentified individual discharged a firearm striking the 21-year-old male victim in the face," police said.

The suspect fled the scene on foot after stealing "an unknown amount of currency," police said.

MANHUNT UNDERWAY FOR KANSAS CITY SHOOTING SPREE SUSPECT NEAR WORLD CUP VENUE; FBI OFFERING $25K REWARD

Authorities noted that the suspect did not take anything else from the vehicle.

Police received reports of the incident at approximately 4:15 a.m.

Emergency Medical Services responded and transported the victim to a local hospital in stable condition. The NYPD also described the shooting as non-fatal.

Authorities are asking for the public’s help identifying the suspect, who was last seen wearing blue shorts, a black sweatshirt, a black surgical mask and black shoes while carrying a black backpack.

Anyone with information about the incident is asked to contact the NYPD Crime Stoppers Hotline at 1-800-577-TIPS (8477). Spanish speakers can call 1-888-57-PISTA (74782). Tips can also be submitted through the NYPD Crime Stoppers website or on X @NYPDTips.

item.title: Journalists demand White House Correspondents' Association 'speak truth to power' in new dinner with Trump
item.description: More than 500 journalists signed a letter urging the WHCA to publicly condemn President Donald Trump's press attacks from the podium at the rescheduled dinner.
item.content:encoded:
More than 500 journalists called on the White House Correspondents' Association to "forcefully stand up" to President Donald Trump on Tuesday ahead of its rescheduled dinner.

"Our message to the White House Correspondents' Association is now more urgent: forcefully stand up for freedom of the press in front of the President, publicly defend the journalists he attacks, and vow to resist his administration’s concerted effort to undermine the First Amendment," a letter, organized by former ABC News correspondent Lisa Stark and former executive producer Ian Cameron, said.

The journalists stated that since the original White House Correspondents' Dinner, the Trump administration has only become more "egregious" in its attacks against the press and encouraged members at the event to publicly condemn the president, who will be in attendance.

WHITE HOUSE CORRESPONDENTS 'EXCITED' FOR TRUMP'S RETURN TO ANNUAL DC DINNER, INSIDERS SAY

"We believe it is hypocritical to celebrate the First Amendment in front of the man who relentlessly attacks it. We urge the WHCA to condemn the administration’s actions from the podium and pledge to fight all attempts by his administration to undermine this core pillar of a functioning democracy," the letter read.

In a statement to Fox News Digital, the WHCA appeared to stand by its plans to host Trump while still honoring the press.

"The White House Correspondents’ Dinner exists because the First Amendment matters most when it’s under pressure. As we continue to celebrate America’s 250th birthday, our choice to gather as journalists, newsmakers and the president in the same room is a reminder of what a free press means to this country — and why it must endure. Not for the media or the president, but for the people who depend on it," the statement read.

FLASHBACK: FIVE OF THE MOST POLITICALLY-CHARGED MOMENTS AT THE WHITE HOUSE CORRESPONDENTS' DINNER

The letter resembled a similar one put out by more than 200 journalists, including former CBS News anchor Dan Rather, in April urging that the dinner "cannot be business as usual."

"We, the undersigned, call upon the White House Correspondents' Association to use the occasion of the White House Correspondents' Dinner, to forcefully demonstrate opposition to President Trump’s efforts to trample freedom of the press," the open letter read. 

"The dinner has long served as a symbol of the vital and irreplaceable role of a free press in American democracy and a celebration of the First Amendment and the journalists who uphold it. President Trump's systematic, sustained, and unprecedented attacks on the free press ... render his presence at such an event a profound contradiction of its purpose."

WHITE HOUSE CORRESPONDENTS' ASSOCIATION PRESIDENT ANNOUNCES EVENT BEING RESCHEDULED AFTER SHOOTING

The original White House Correspondents' Dinner ended abruptly April 25 at the Washington Hilton when an armed suspect attempted to storm the event in what authorities say was an assassination attempt against Trump. The event was then rescheduled for July 24 at the Waldorf Astoria.

This year's White House Correspondents' Dinner is the first time Trump has attended the annual event as president after previously skipping it throughout his first term. He previously attended the dinner as a private citizen in 2011.

item.title: Sports media rushes to cast Ryan Clark as victim while portraying ESPN as the villain
item.description: Ryan Clark's ESPN dismissal sparked outrage from Stephen A. Smith and Jemele Hill, but his history of apologies and friction tells a different story.
item.content:encoded:
Within hours of Ryan Clark’s dismissal, many of the biggest names in sports media had already reached the same conclusion: Clark was the victim and ESPN was the villain.

Stephen A. Smith said he confronted ESPN executives over the decision and called Clark "arguably the best NFL analyst in America." Emmanuel Acho accused the network of handling the situation in a "cowardly" and "wildly disrespectful" manner.

RYAN CLARK BECAME A HEADACHE FOR ESPN

Sarah Spain described Clark as "another incredibly smart, thoughtful, nuanced talent let go," while Pro Football Talk’s Mike Florio declared that Clark took "the high road" after "ESPN took the low road."

Jemele Hill went even further.

Hill suggested Clark had "a hater," perhaps multiple haters, inside ESPN. She attacked OutKick as a "disgusting" right-wing "rag" and theorized that someone within the network intentionally leaked Clark’s dismissal to OutKick in order to create a conservative media narrative against him. She offered no evidence for that claim.

But the fact that Clark’s dismissal leaked so widely and so quickly raises a question that his defenders have mostly avoided: How many enemies had he made inside ESPN?

Much of the criticism centered on the fact that Clark was informed during a live edition of NFL Live. The assumption was that ESPN knowingly allowed a longtime employee to begin the show before abruptly firing him during a break.

I don’t think that’s what happened.

I think ESPN planned to inform Clark later and simply failed to anticipate that news of his dismissal would leak first. Once OutKick contacted the network with a confirmed story, ESPN realized it had lost control of the timeline and had to tell Clark before he learned about it publicly.

That’s not cruelty. It’s poor planning.

My colleague Bobby Burack and I developed separate lines of reporting that the network was preparing to part ways with Clark. Four sources confirmed the information. Despite concerns that alerting ESPN could jeopardize the story, I contacted the network’s communications department at 4 p.m. and asked ESPN to confirm or deny the report before our publication deadline.

ESPN asked us to wait until 5:45 p.m. while it was "trying to chase this down." We declined but told the network we would add anything it provided before that time. It's reasonable that ESPN wanted more time to inform Clark before the news became public.

At 4:49 p.m., ESPN unexpectedly told us we could "stick to" our original timeline. Fourteen minutes later, Andrew Marchand published a brief report in The Athletic. OutKick’s more detailed story followed one minute after that.

The most logical explanation is that ESPN believed it still had time to manage Clark’s exit quietly. Once OutKick informed the network that four sources had already confirmed the decision, executives were forced to accelerate a conversation they apparently had not planned to have during NFL Live.

ESPN’s mistake was not deciding to embarrass Clark on live television. It was failing to anticipate that the information would leak before the network was ready to tell him.

That raises a more uncomfortable question than the ones Clark’s defenders have been asking: Why were so many people inside or around ESPN willing to talk about his dismissal?

Hill may be correct that Clark had "haters" inside the company.

What she never seriously considered, or chose to ignore, is how he might have acquired them.

I worked at ESPN for years and spent time in rooms with Clark. He appeared on several shows I produced. The thing I remember most clearly is how uncomfortable he could make those rooms.

Clark often carried himself as though he were the smartest person present, and he made sure everyone knew it. That dynamic was especially difficult for producers and other employees beneath him in ESPN’s hierarchy. There was little room to challenge him or offer feedback that amounted to anything other than telling him he was great.

I distinctly remember one NFL season when talent, producers and other ESPN employees gathered on Sunday afternoons to watch the games in a large conference room. There were usually 25 or 30 people there. The mood was light. Chris Berman often sat near the front and cracked jokes throughout the afternoon.

Clark joined the group one Sunday and sat in the back with his arms folded. Several times, someone offered an opinion about a game and Clark scoffed. Eventually, most people stopped talking. I left and watched the games from a control room because he had completely ruined the experience for me.

How dare anyone offer an opinion when the world’s greatest NFL analyst was available to explain what everyone else was apparently too stupid to understand?

That was one afternoon years ago. It does not prove why ESPN fired Clark, nor does it mean every person who worked with him had the same experience.

It does, however, make the speed of the leak less surprising to me.

People rarely risk their relationships with a company to leak sensitive personnel news about a universally beloved colleague.

Clark's defenders largely presented his dismissal as though it happened in a vacuum.

It didn't.

Clark was forced to apologize after making inaccurate claims on ESPN about the investigation into former LSU receiver Kyren Lacy’s role in a fatal crash. He presented claims from Lacy’s attorney as established fact and accused law enforcement of wrongdoing before Louisiana State Police released additional evidence that contradicted key parts of the story Clark told viewers.

He later acknowledged that he had failed to provide complete and accurate information.

Clark also admitted on ESPN that he had argued in favor of Bryce Young over C.J. Stroud despite privately believing Stroud was the superior quarterback. His explanation was that Young needed someone to support him publicly.

That’s a remarkable admission from an analyst whose job is supposed to be giving viewers his honest evaluation.

ESPN'S RYAN CLARK WANTS TO BE AN NFL GM, LIES ABOUT C.J. STROUD & BRYCE YOUNG TO PROVE HE CAN DO IT

Then there was Clark’s public feud with Robert Griffin III, during which he suggested Griffin was not qualified to discuss Black women because he is married to a White woman. The dispute became deeply personal and reportedly frustrated people inside ESPN. This led to another Clark apology.

Then there was Clark’s on-air confrontation with Peter Schrager that became another viral controversy. Clark later apologized (again), but the exchange likely contributed to concerns among network executives.

Plus, Clark’s difficult relationship with ESPN management was hardly limited to interpersonal friction.

His 2024 contract negotiations became a public spectacle. Clark allowed his deal to expire, posted videos about his status and openly applied pressure to the network while seeking a substantial raise. ESPN eventually brought him back on a deal reportedly worth around $2 million per year, but the process was contentious.

ESPN executives do not enjoy being publicly cornered during contract negotiations, particularly by employees using their own platforms to rally outside pressure against the company.

My belief is that Clark’s 2024 extension was always going to be his final contract with ESPN. The network may have decided that keeping him temporarily was preferable to another public fight at the time, while also concluding it had no desire to negotiate with him again.

That would help explain why ESPN was willing to move on despite his high salary and recent extension. The decision may not have been prompted by one argument, one apology or one uncomfortable television segment. It may have been the culmination of a relationship that had already run its course.

Essentially, Clark’s tenure had become more complicated than his defenders acknowledged.

Spain praised him as "thoughtful" and "nuanced" without mentioning the multiple inaccurate claims that forced him to apologize on national television. Hill assumed the existence of internal enemies proved something nefarious without examining Clark’s own role in creating friction. Smith emphasized Clark’s talent, which was never really in dispute.

Acho offered perhaps the most exaggerated defense, claiming Clark helped build NFL Live into the "juggernaut it is today."

Acho pointed to the show’s Sports Emmy victories as evidence. But awards demonstrate that people within the television industry and the Hollywood elites like a program. They do not necessarily demonstrate that the public at large enjoys a show.

The available audience data show that NFL Live drew larger audiences before Clark became one of the faces of the program than it has in recent years. ESPN reported that the show averaged 688,000 viewers during a comparable early-season period in 2013 and 624,000 in 2012. It averaged roughly 486,000 in 2015, 492,000 in 2016, 449,000 in 2021 and 440,000 in 2025.

That doesn't mean Clark hurt NFL Live. It does make the claim that he "built" a show that debuted in 1998 into a modern ratings juggernaut impossible to support.

The argument becomes even more amusing when compared with Acho’s commentary about Caitlin Clark. He has argued that the WNBA would be better off without her, even though she has driven enormous gains in television audiences, attendance and general interest.

By Acho’s standard, Caitlin Clark has a much stronger claim to having "built" the modern WNBA than Ryan Clark does to having built NFL Live.

Apparently, the meaning of the word depends on which Clark is receiving the credit.

By the time ESPN reached this week’s layoffs, Clark was an expensive employee who had publicly pressured the company during his previous contract negotiation, accumulated enemies inside the building and repeatedly created controversies outside it.

ESPN TO PART WAYS WITH CAM NEWTON AS PART OF TALENT LAYOFFS THAT ALSO INCLUDED RYAN CLARK

The sports media narrative was that ESPN betrayed Ryan Clark.

OutKick's reporting suggested something different.

ESPN didn’t lose control of the story because OutKick uncovered some closely guarded secret. It lost control because too many people already knew.

And too many people were willing to talk.

That doesn’t happen by accident.

item.title: William Shatner and daughter Melanie reveal they battled Stage 4 cancer at the same time
item.description: Melanie Shatner Gretsch reveals how a late-night lump led to her HER2-positive breast cancer diagnosis while her father faced Stage 4 melanoma.
item.content:encoded:
William Shatner and his 61-year-old daughter revealed this week that the two of them battled cancer at the same time several years ago.

Melanie Shatner Gretsch first discovered her cancer four years ago when she woke up in the middle of the night and felt a little lump on her breast she thought might be a bug bite.

She told People magazine she decided to have it checked out despite a mammogram not finding anything just five months earlier.

DAVE COULIER REVEALS 45-POUND WEIGHT LOSS, GRUELING SIDE EFFECTS FROM CANCER FIGHTS

Although the technician didn’t find anything during a scan the next day, "something pushed me back and said, ‘No, have her recheck it.’ She put the wand exactly where I put my finger and under her breath said, ‘Now, I would have missed that.’"

She was diagnosed with HER2-positive breast cancer that had spread to lymph nodes in her chest.

Shatner said his daughter was "very conscious of the importance of her life for herself and for her family. I was devastated."

Gretsch is a mother to two daughters she shares with husband Joel Gretsch.

Gretsch said her father told her not to worry about the medical costs and to send him the bills.

"Then you made me laugh so hard when I was still going to the doctor a year later," she added. "You said, ‘You’ve got to get well. We’re going to be in the poorhouse.’"

LIKE WHAT YOU’RE READING? CLICK HERE FOR MORE ENTERTAINMENT NEWS

For the next 18 months, her life was filled with multiple rounds of chemo, 30 radiation treatments and a double mastectomy.

"Chemo was the most physically draining, and the radiation I found so emotionally draining," she admitted.

Just as she was nearing the end of her treatments, a lump appeared on her dad’s cheek.

He said a family doctor told him it was just a blocked salivary gland that was nothing to worry about, but a doctor friend whose advice he asked while they were horseback riding advised him to get it removed.

CAMERON MATHISON SAYS NOTICING SUBTLE BODY CHANGES ‘SAVED MY LIFE’ AFTER CANCER DIAGNOSIS

"He placed his hands on my cheeks, and he said with love in his voice, ‘You better get this out,’" Shatner explained.

In June 2023, Shatner was diagnosed with Stage 4 malignant melanoma that had spread to his lungs and brain.

"I remember vividly thinking, ‘I don’t have the strength to take care of myself and lose my father at the same time,’" Gretsch admitted.

Shatner was more upbeat. 

"They said, ‘You got cancer. If you came in later, you would have been dead.’ See? That’s my life. I think the universe is taking care of me."

The 95-year-old had the tumor removed and underwent two years of immunotherapy.

CLICK HERE TO SIGN UP FOR THE ENTERTAINMENT NEWSLETTER

"He wouldn’t stop his life," his daughter told People magazine. "He kept working and moving forward. Whenever he was home, Joel and I would bring dinner to him and watch movies, just always made sure that he’s not alone."

Shatner added, "We got the perfect doctors for her cancer and my cancer."

The "Star Trek" actor, who was the oldest man in space when he went up in a Blue Origin rocket, has stayed busy with two new books and two records, all expected to come out next year.

"I’m healthier now than I’ve been in years," Shatner said. "The brain is like a muscle. It feeds on activity."

Near the end of 2024, both were declared cancer free.

"It was the third time in my life I had heard him cry," Gretsch admitted of telling him her cancer was gone.

Shatner said the experience has given him a new perspective on life.

"I’m thinking of the miracle of a hummingbird, and of my dog taking a ball and my children," he said. "I’m aware of the magic of it all."

item.title: Fox News Poll: Voters want major change amid economic and political discontent
item.description: Trump approval holds at 39% as Democrats lead the generic ballot by 7 points, the highest for either party in Fox News polling since 1996.
item.content:encoded:
Several findings in the latest Fox News Poll show voters are unhappy and want major change, which bodes well for Democrats in November: President Donald Trump’s job approval remains near a record low, more Democrats are motivated to vote, Republicans join Democrats and independents in rating the economy negatively, and 4 in 10 voters want "dramatic" or "revolutionary" change. Plus, more view the GOP as tied to elites than the Democratic Party.

Voters say Democrats can better handle income inequality (+22 points), healthcare (+21), inflation (+10), the economy (+9), and foreign policy (+7).  Republicans hold the advantage on crime (+7 points), while government spending (Democrats +3) and national security (R +2) are more evenly divided. On immigration, the GOP had an 8-point edge in April. Now, Democrats are preferred by one point. 

FOX NEWS POLL: AN EARLY LOOK AT THE GEORGIA SENATE RACE

More Democrats (68%) say they are extremely motivated to vote in November than Republicans (57%) by an 11-point margin. By a 25-point spread, more MAGA Republicans (66%) are extremely motivated than non-MAGA Republicans (41%).

FOX NEWS POLL: A CLOSE SENATE CONTEST IS BREWING IN IOWA

If the election were today, 53% of voters would back the Democratic candidate in their House district and 46% the Republican. That 7-point edge is just outside the poll’s margin of error. Democrats were up by 5 points in April. Notably, 53% is the highest percentage either party has received on the generic House ballot in Fox News polling since 1996.

When looking only at counties where the 2024 presidential vote was close — Trump and former Vice President Kamala Harris within 10 points — the Democratic candidate leads by 59-41%.

"Although these data look promising for the Democrats, some of their support is in less-contested districts, padding their national totals without adding House seats," says Republican pollster Daron Shaw, who conducts Fox News polls with Democrat Chris Anderson. "The best estimates I’ve seen suggest the GOP could retain the House even if the Democrats win the national vote by 2-3 points, but if Democrats win by 6-7 points, they probably take the House."

The Democratic advantage comes amid weak ratings for Trump and continued economic pessimism. Trump’s job approval stands at 39%, unchanged for the third month in a row and one point above his low of 38% in October 2017. Approval of Trump is a touch lower than former President Barack Obama’s at the same point in his second term (42% in July 2014).

Trump received net-negative ratings for handling immigration (42% approve, 57% disapprove), Iran (34-66%), the economy (33-67%), and inflation (27-73%).

Three-quarters rate the economy negatively, about the same for the past six months.

Another three quarters say inflation has caused them financial hardship, including 34% who say "serious" hardship, up from 26% last year.

Fifty-four percent expect the economy to worsen in the coming year; only Republicans express optimism (71%), while Democrats (75%) and independents (64%) expect it to worsen.

Dissatisfaction extends beyond individual figures to both political parties and the broader economic system.

"Democrats and Republicans have a surprisingly broad array of shared beliefs about politics today," says Shaw. "Both think elected officials answer to the elites, both think the system is rigged, both want to see new blood in office, both feel betrayed by their party, both disapprove of their members in Congress, and both want a mandatory retirement age for Congress."

Some 70% say the Republican Party is closely tied to elite interests, while 55% say the same about the Democratic Party. The 15-point gap reflects more Democrats and independents saying the GOP is tied to elites, yet even Republicans are equally likely to describe both parties as close to elites.

There’s disillusionment in both parties. Many Democrats (48%) and Republicans (41%) say they feel betrayed by their party, and for both it is primarily for selling out to special interests.

Perceptions that the political system favors elites are reshaping views of the economy as well. The number who accept income inequality because "that’s how our economy works" has fallen 16 points compared to 2014 (from 62% to 46%), while the share who want government action has more than doubled (from 13% to 30%).

At the same time, an increasing number, 65%, think the country’s system is rigged in favor of the wealthy (up 9 points compared to 2019). 

By 2-to-1, voters say it is more important to elect new people than keep those with experience.

Overall, 19% say they want revolutionary change in the nation’s government, while many others want substantial (38%) or dramatic (23%) change.

Almost twice as many Democrats (52%) as Republicans (28%) want dramatic or revolutionary change. 

Nearly 7 in 10 voters disapprove of both Democrats and Republicans in Congress.

Some 32% of Republicans disapprove of their party’s lawmakers, while 39% of Democrats disapprove of theirs. More than 8 in 10 independents disapprove of both.

Fully 85% support a mandatory retirement age for members of Congress, up 20 points since 2002.

Of those in favor of setting a cap, 80% think it should be before age 75, with 61% selecting a cutoff between 65 and 74 — a boundary supported by more than half of every generation.

Just 50% of voters have a positive view of capitalism, down from a high of 57% in 2019. For socialism, it’s 30% favorable, up from 27% last year but below the 32% who said the same in 2021. The Democratic Socialists of America are viewed positively by 31%.

By an 11-point margin, more Democrats have a favorable opinion of socialism (52%) than capitalism (41%), while by a 50-point margin, more Republicans have a positive view of capitalism (61%) than socialism (11%).

Forty-five percent of voters have a favorable view of Vermont Sen. Bernie Sanders, I-Vt., That makes him more popular than Vice President JD Vance (41% favorable), Trump (38%), and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, D-N.Y., (35%).  Each is viewed more unfavorably than favorably. Voters under 30 and those ages 65+ are equally likely to view Sanders positively (48% and 46% respectively). For AOC, more voters 65+ (41%) have a favorable view than young voters (33%), and three times as many of those under 30 say they’ve never heard of her (30% vs. 9%).

Overall, 44% have a favorable view of Israel, down from 58% a decade ago. Positive ratings are lower today among Democrats (by 14 points), Republicans (-12), and independents (-21). Voters ages 65 and over (56%) are twice as likely to view Israel favorably as those under 30 (27%).

CLICK HERE FOR CROSSTABS AND TOPLINE

Conducted July 17–20, 2026, under the direction of Beacon Research (D) and Shaw & Company Research (R), this Fox News survey includes interviews with a sample of 1,003 registered voters randomly selected from a national voter file. Respondents spoke with live interviewers on landlines (70) and cellphones (681) or completed the survey online after receiving a text (252). Results based on the full sample have a margin of sampling error of ±3 percentage points. The survey includes an oversample of 200 voters aged 18–29, bringing the sample size for that subgroup to 350 (±5). Sampling error for results among subgroups is higher. In addition to sampling errors, question wording and order can influence results. Weights are generally applied to age, race, education, and area variables to ensure the demographics are representative of the registered voter population. Sources for developing weight targets include the most recent American Community Survey, Fox News Voter Analysis, and voter file data.

Fox News’ Victoria Balara contributed to this report.

item.title: Migrant allegedly kills 3 apartment building staffers over parking lot contract dispute
item.description: Tsegaab Binessu, a migrant from Ethiopia and a former truck driver, allegedly killed three St. Paul apartment staffers over a parking cancellation dispute.
item.content:encoded:
An African migrant upset over being unable to cancel his monthly $65 parking garage contract allegedly shot and killed three property managers at his Minnesota apartment building over the dispute, authorities said.

Tsegaab Ademassu Binessu, 30, was charged with three counts of second-degree murder with intent following the alleged slayings Monday of Nansi G. Fuentes Zambrano, 25, Dena M. Silcox, 56, and 43-year-old Adam J. Wilwerding at the St. Paul complex, court documents state.

The rampage began after Binessu, from Ethiopia, called the apartment building staffers to cancel his $65 monthly parking contract because he had sold his car. He was told he could not do so after recently renewing the agreement.

Despite being told the contract could not be canceled, Binessu said he would be going to the management office in person, prosecutors said.

EXCLUSIVE: DHS SEEKS CUSTODY OF ILLEGAL IMMIGRANT CHARGED WITH MURDER AFTER TRUMP DEPORTATION, BIDEN RELEASE

Once there, he allegedly opened fire, killing all three victims. Police later found 21, 9 mm shell casings in the leasing office.

Shortly after the shooting, a man called the police stating that he had been Binessu's roommate since 2018. He said Binessu, who had aspirations of joining the U.S. Navy, called him and confessed to the triple murder, adding that he was walking to the police station.

Binessu worked as a trucker but quit two months ago, the roommate told authorities, and had planned on enlisting in the military. He sold his car the same day as the shooting, police said.

The roommate said he spoke with Binessu, who allegedly said he "argued with apartment management and ended up killing three people."

FAMILY SHATTERED AFTER 3-TIME DEPORTED ILLEGAL IMMIGRANT ALLEGEDLY BLEW THROUGH STOP SIGN, ROILING HOUSE RACE

Surveillance footage reviewed by investigators captured Binessu talking with Silcox and Wilwerding.

At some point, Binessu allegedly pulled up his shirt, grabbed his gun from his waistband, pointed the weapon at Wilwerding, who put his hands in front of his body, and shot him three times.

Binessu then fired three more times as Wilwerding fell to the floor, court documents said.

Silcox screamed and covered her ears when Binessu allegedly shot Wilwerding, saying, "No, no — no, no, no."

Binessu is then accused of shooting Silcox five times in rapid succession, according to court documents. As she wheeled her chair away from the desk, Binessu allegedly shot her a sixth time.

TRUMP ADMINISTRATION PLANS NEW ICE HOLDING FACILITY NEAR LOUISIANA DEPORTATION FLIGHT HUB

Binessu stalked around the office and said, "Motherf------ b----," court documents said. He then moved toward a hallway out of the camera's view, where a woman's screams could be heard followed by four more gunshots.

Prosecutors said Binessu was arrested while driving Fuentes Zambrano's car, which contained what appeared to be newly purchased camping gear, according to the charges. Inside the vehicle, authorities found his U.S. passport, Social Security card, an empty handgun magazine and his gun permit.

During an interview with investigators, Binessu allegedly voiced frustration with the "prolonged immigration process" and not being able to bring his wife and children to the United States for seven years, which he said contributed to his divorce and separation from his children.

"Binessu believes he was treated unfairly because he is Black and an immigrant," the documents said. "Binessu repeatedly said he has lost everything he cared about: his wife, his children, his business, his peace of mind. Binessu said life without his family no longer made sense."

He remains in police custody.

item.title: Spanish soccer players got a bizarre reward for winning the World Cup that'll probably spoil in a week or two
item.description: World Cup winners Fabian Ruiz and Gavi each received their body weight in tomatoes from their Spanish hometown of Los Palacios y Villafranca.
item.content:encoded:
The great thing about winning a major tournament like the World Cup isn't just the trophy itself; it's all the other stuff that comes with it.

Prize money, endorsements, the adulation of millions across your nation.

And, in the case of two Spanish soccer players, the win has scored them a metric assload of tomatoes.

SPAIN BEATS ARGENTINA TO WIN 2026 FIFA WORLD CUP BEHIND FERRAN TORRES' EXTRA-TIME GOAL

Midfielders Fabian Ruiz and Gavi both hail from the city of Los Palacios y Villafranca, which has a tradition of rewarding champions with tomatoes.

According to UPI, the province that the city is in, Seville, is known for tomato production, but it doesn't just give out a commemorative tomato on a plaque. Instead, the two champs had to hop on scales at an event at a town hall, because each of them was being rewarded with their weight in tomatoes.

Ruiz left with 187 pounds of the fruit (I know, I think it should be a vegetable too, but you have to trust the science) while Gavi's supply is a little smaller at 151 pounds.

I'm torn on this one. I like the idea of towns, states, cities and provinces rewarding people with their weight in whatever their greatest export is.

This is mainly because under these rules, if I did anything good enough, I'd have a lot of Lebanon bologna (how many pounds is not important, just know that it would be plenty).

But, on the other hand, aren't like 130 pounds of those tomatoes just going to go bad?

WORLD CUP HERO FERRAN TORRES DOUBLES DOWN AFTER CRITICISM, POSTS PICTURE WEARING 'MAKE SPAIN GREAT AGAIN' HAT

And where do you store them? I know it's the thought that counts, but if someone said to you, "Hey, here's 200 pounds of tomatoes, champ," you'd be like, "I guess I can keep some of them in the guest bathroom tub..."

You have to just give them away. That happened to me with a giant case of Tastykakes that I once happened to receive (long story). Everyone who came by my house for anything left with a box of Butterscotch Krimpets.

Nonetheless, it's a cool tradition, and I think there is going to be a lot of pan con tomate (which is delicious) in Ruiz and Gavi's future.

item.title: ICE urges North Carolina to keep illegal immigrant charged in triple-fatal hit-and-run behind bars
item.description: ICE has lodged a detainer for Javiel Pena Sola, an illegal immigrant charged in a deadly Salisbury hit-and-run that killed three people on July 18.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) is asking authorities in North Carolina to keep an illegal immigrant charged in a deadly hit-and-run that killed three people behind bars.

Javiel Pena Sola, a Salvadoran citizen, allegedly struck and killed three people traveling in a vehicle on July 18 in Salisbury before he fled the scene. He was found with the help of a police K-9, ICE said.

Two victims were pronounced dead at the scene, while a third was airlifted to a hospital where they later died.

MAN CHARGED IN CRASH THAT KILLED MOTHER AND TWO YOUNG SONS HAS ICE DETAINER, PRIOR DWI CONVICTIONS

"I heard people screaming ... and they pulled the man out; and as they pulled him out, he started screaming," witness Quindel Woods told WSOC-TV.

Sola's vehicle was later found in a creek. He is charged with three counts of hit-and-run involving serious injury or death.

ICE has lodged a detainer with local authorities to keep him in custody.

EXCLUSIVE: DHS SEEKS CUSTODY OF ILLEGAL IMMIGRANT CHARGED WITH MURDER AFTER TRUMP DEPORTATION, BIDEN RELEASE

Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Assistant Secretary Lauren Bis said Sola was arrested in 2016 but was released. She said he entered the United States through Arizona in April 2022.

"Although ICE had arrested him in 2016, the Obama Administration RELEASED him into American communities," she said. "These deaths were 100% preventable. Open borders and sanctuary policies have deadly consequences."

Sola's criminal history includes prior convictions for reckless endangerment, failure to stop after an accident involving damage to an attended vehicle, and driving without a license in Maryland in July 2015, as well as an arrest for escaping from custody.

After his arrest in Maryland on charges of escaping from custody, law enforcement officials in Baltimore honored an ICE detainer and turned him over to federal agents in March 2016. He was then released by the Obama administration two months later, ICE said.

item.title: Nick Saban has the best solution for fixing the problems with college football scheduling
item.description: Nick Saban says college football teams should play 12 Power 5 games, eliminating easy non-conference schedules that weaken strength of schedule.
item.content:encoded:
Almost everyone acknowledges that college football is at a turning point. Whether it be name, image and likeness, issues with paying players directly, expansion of the College Football Playoff, conferences breaking off from the NCAA, or debates over strength of schedule.

All these issues have had a significant impact on where the sport currently is and where it's going. Some in good ways, some in bad.

One of the most unequivocally bad outcomes? Teams decreasing the quality of their schedules because it's easier to win more games and make the College Football Playoff. Look no further than the collapse of the USC-Notre Dame rivalry game for the foreseeable future.

FLORIDA STATE-GEORGIA SERIES CANCELLATION IS OMINOUS SIGN FOR FUTURE OF COLLEGE FOOTBALL

Both sides "accidentally" benefit from the game's cancellation. One less top-15 opponent, one more likely win to sell to the College Football Playoff selection committee. And therein lies the problem; because making the playoff is viewed as the most important goal by coaches, fans, and athletic directors, the incentives are tilted in that direction. Which is how we've gotten where we are: many Power 4 teams setting up easy non-conference schedules.

Well, the best head coach in the sport's history, Nick Saban, has a solution. Just man up and play big opponents all season.

"I actually think we should play 12 games against the Power 5," Saban said on Wednesday, per On3's Brett McMurphy. "That's it. Because it's a business and people want to see those games."

NICK SABAN URGES SENATE TO PASS LEGISLATION TO FIX COLLEGE SPORTS, BUT COACHING CONTRACTS GLOSSED OVER

How refreshing is that? Yes. College football is a business. People want to see their favorite teams play other big teams, because the games are more exciting, interesting, and meaningful. Who would argue with seeing more games between the Big Ten and the SEC? Well, SEC coaches who enjoy complaining about non-conference schedules and ensure their teams never leave the central time zone, cough Missouri.

Or the ACC and Big Ten? Or Big 12 and SEC? Obviously that would mean more losses, but if the playoff is headed for 24 teams anyway, so what?

As an example, here's the week one schedule for some big name teams:

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

Then there's Ole Miss-Louisville, Florida State-SMU, Cal-UCLA, Washington-Washington State, and Wisconsin-Notre Dame. Games of the year? No. But more exciting and interesting than a parade of G5 teams? Absolutely. You want ratings, and attendance? Prioritize better games.

And therein lies the brilliance of Saban's solution. Just play better games, trust your team, and let the chips fall where they may. It'll never happen, but it should.

item.title: Spanberger's ICE rollback to blame for toddler killing, Virginia 'hotbed' of illegal immigrant crime: DHS
item.description: DHS says Gov. Abigail Spanberger's rollback of Virginia's 287(g) agreement created a "hotbed" for illegal immigrant crime after a toddler's killing.
item.content:encoded:
FIRST ON FOX: The Department of Homeland Security blasted Virginia Gov. Abigail Spanberger after a red county’s cooperation with the agency led to swift justice for an illegal immigrant accused of killing a child.

DHS accused Spanberger of creating a "hotbed" for illegal immigrant violence with her day one order rescinding former Gov. Glenn Youngkin’s 287(g) agreement, which cut cooperative ties between ICE and Virginia State Police, fish and game wardens and state corrections officials, after the latest gruesome incident.
